title: phlox longifolia subsp. cortezana
intro: Phlox longifolia subsp. cortezana, also known as Cortez's phlox, is a perennial flowering plant native to the southwestern United States. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Cortez's phlox can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for various applications in traditional medicine.
